GitHub

Analysis And Transitions

These helpers operate on lookup-enabled sweeps and expose transition analysis plus dispersive summaries.

ScQubitsMimic.transitions — Function
transitions(slice::SweepSlice; as_specdata=false, subsystems=nothing,
            initial=nothing, final=nothing, sidebands=false,
            photon_number=1, make_positive=false)

Compute transition energies across a one-dimensional SweepSlice.

By default this returns (transition_specs, transition_energies). With as_specdata=true, it returns a named tuple containing the swept parameter axis, a transition-energy table, and human-readable labels.

source
transitions(sweep::ParameterSweep; kwargs...)

Convenience wrapper for one-dimensional sweeps. Internally this applies the full sweep slice and forwards to transitions(::SweepSlice; ...).

source
ScQubitsMimic.chi_matrix — Function
chi_matrix(hs::HilbertSpace) -> Matrix{Float64}

Cross-Kerr (dispersive shift) matrix between all pairs of subsystems.

χ_ij = E(1_i,1_j) - E(1_i,0_j) - E(0_i,1_j) + E(0_i,0_j)

where E(ni, nj) denotes the dressed energy with ni excitations in subsystem i and nj in subsystem j (all other subsystems in ground state).

Bare labels are 1-based: ground = 1, first excited = 2, etc.

Requires generate_lookup!(hs) to have been called first.

source
chi_matrix(sweep::ParameterSweep) -> Array{Float64, 3}

Chi matrix at each sweep point. Returns array of size (n_points, n_sub, n_sub). Requires lookup data to be present on the sweep, either because it was created with store_lookups=true or because generate_lookup!(sweep) was called after run!(sweep). The result depends only on the lookups stored on the sweep itself; if strong hybridization requires relaxed bare-label tracking, construct the sweep with ignore_low_overlap=true.

source
ScQubitsMimic.self_kerr — Function
self_kerr(hs::HilbertSpace, subsys_idx::Int) -> Float64

Self-Kerr (anharmonicity) of subsystem subsys_idx in the dressed basis:

K = E(2) - 2·E(1) + E(0)

where E(n) is the dressed energy with n excitations in subsys_idx, all other subsystems in ground state. Bare labels are 1-based.

Requires generate_lookup!(hs) to have been called first.

source
self_kerr(sweep::ParameterSweep, subsys_idx::Int) -> Vector{Float64}

Self-Kerr at each sweep point. Requires lookup data on the sweep. Uses the sweep-stored lookup policy, not sweep.hilbertspace.ignore_low_overlap.

source
ScQubitsMimic.lamb_shift — Function
lamb_shift(hs::HilbertSpace, subsys_idx::Int) -> Float64

Lamb shift of subsystem subsys_idx: difference between dressed and bare transition frequency (0→1).

Δω = (E_dressed(1) - E_dressed(0)) - (E_bare(1) - E_bare(0))

Requires generate_lookup!(hs) to have been called first.

source
lamb_shift(sweep::ParameterSweep, subsys_idx::Int) -> Vector{Float64}

Lamb shift at each sweep point. Requires lookup data on the sweep. Uses the sweep-stored lookup policy, not sweep.hilbertspace.ignore_low_overlap.

source
